portfolio_rationale: LLY stands out as the highest-conviction pick with the cleanest bull case: 54% revenue growth, no bearish fundamental factors, all-green technical indicators (RSI not overbought), rising search trends, and a consistent 3-week bullish signal history. AMZN earns second place with strong momentum confirmation and a bullish technical reversal (price now above 20-day MA, MACD positive), though news trends are mixed. AAPL is third with solid fundamentals and positive momentum but carries overbought risk (RSI 80+) and a recent history of neutral judge signals. GOOGL has an extremely overbought RSI (84.7) and a 46% drop in search trends, making the bull case less convincing despite the judge's bullish signal. XOM has a weaker fundamental profile (limited metrics available, older data) and energy sector macro headwinds. BRK.B receives zero weight as the judge explicitly ruled bearish.

LLY.reason: Strongest bull case in the universe: 54% revenue growth, clean technicals with no overbought signal, rising search interest, and three consecutive bullish judge decisions with high confidence.

AMZN.reason: Strong fundamental metrics, confirmed technical momentum reversal with price above 20-day MA and MACD positive, and bullish judge at 0.85 confidence, though mixed news trends limit sizing.

AAPL.reason: Solid profitability and cash flow with bullish judge at 0.85, but RSI at 80.5 signals overbought conditions and recent signal history shows two prior neutral calls, warranting a moderate position.

GOOGL.reason: Bullish judge at 0.85 confidence with strong ROE and positive technicals, but RSI at 84.7 is extremely overbought and Google Trends dropped 46% week-over-week, limiting conviction.

XOM.reason: Consistent three-week bullish signal history and positive technicals, but fundamental data is stale (August 2025) and the bull case rests on limited metrics, warranting a small position.

BRK.B.reason: Judge signal is explicitly bearish at 0.85 confidence with negative revenue growth, multiple bearish technical indicators, and a bear case that clearly outargued the bull case.
